rationale: In the citing paper, this reference is not just listed in a citation cluster; it is discussed in a dedicated sentence with its core finding and mechanism (specific goals help under why focus but hurt under how focus because perceived difficulty increases). The citation is used in the General Discussion to interpret and position the article's contribution within a related stream on concrete planning and difficulty perceptions in self-regulation. Although it is not central to the paper's hypotheses or design, it plays a meaningful conceptual positioning role.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Ulkumen and Cheema (2011) as prior evidence that concrete, how-focused goal framing can backfire in savings by increasing perceived difficulty. It then presents its own duration-framing findings as adding to that broader literature on difficulty perceptions hindering goal attainment.
